title: Early pregnancy symptoms

red_flags.text: Defines the up-to-13-week complication population and services offering specialist assessment, ultrasound, serum hCG review, and timely referral.
red_flags.rationale: Supports the complication-focused early-pregnancy assessment context, ultrasound and hCG service review, and clinician-arranged escalation.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines the up-to-13-week complication population and services offering specialist assessment, ultrasound, serum hCG review, and timely referral.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management

exact_section.heading: Overview and recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.4 — early pregnancy assessment services

red_flags.text: Lists pain, amenorrhoea, bleeding, gastrointestinal symptoms, dizziness or syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, tenderness, haemodynamic signs, pregnancy testing, and escalation criteria.
red_flags.rationale: Directly supports mapped pain, bleeding, dizziness, syncope, shoulder-tip pain, pregnancy-test, haemodynamic, and tenderness items.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists pain, amenorrhoea, bleeding, gastrointestinal symptoms, dizziness or syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, tenderness, haemodynamic signs, pregnancy testing, and escalation criteria.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management

follow_up.text: Defines clinician-performed transvaginal or selected transabdominal ultrasound and cautions against overinterpreting one early scan or LMP alone.
follow_up.rationale: Directly supports mapped ultrasound review and cautions against relying on LMP or a single early scan alone.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Defines clinician-performed transvaginal or selected transabdominal ultrasound and cautions against overinterpreting one early scan or LMP alone.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.7.6 — ultrasound for pregnancy location and viability

follow_up.text: Requires symptoms to take priority over hCG, limits hCG interpretation to appropriate clinical use, and requires clear worsening-symptom access and follow-up.
follow_up.rationale: Directly supports mapped hCG review, worsening-symptom warnings, and clinician-arranged follow-up without autonomous interpretation.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Requires symptoms to take priority over hCG, limits hCG interpretation to appropriate clinical use, and requires clear worsening-symptom access and follow-up.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.8.1–1.8.10 — hCG in pregnancy of unknown location

source_limitations: Uncomplicated nausea, vomiting, fatigue, urinary frequency, dehydration, CBC, urine dip, and medication management remain unsupported by the reviewed sections.
Threatened miscarriage and viability-query labels must not be inferred from symptoms.
UAE referral pathways and qualified clinician review remain unresolved.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
